item.title: PROBLEM
item.content:encoded:
I love this article, but I found one teensy-weensy problem.

  • SCP-4255 says he hasn't been born yet
  • SCP-4255 is around in 2069
  • SCP-4255 is sixty to seventy years old

Those three conditions cannot simultaneously exist. May I suggest changing it to 2089 or 2099?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to like this article, but the grammatical errors break the flow too much for me to enjoy it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: for me bootstrap paradoxes ruin time travel stories because they don't have a reason to end up that way, it makes it feel like some sort of plot device rather than time travel. I still really enjoyed this, the bootstrap paradox didn't get in the way I just thought I'd mention it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
although the bootstrap paradox is quite a big hole in the article,

The entire article is a bootstrap paradox. That's the point. The Foundation had to make Santa Claus in the future because in the past there was evidence to suggest they would have to make Santa Claus. The only reason they made Santa Claus is because they were told by Santa Claus that they were responsible for him existing based on evidence from the interview.

It's a massive migraine inducing paradox.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
so if I'm getting this right, the foundation in the future created Santa Claus in order to prevent a paradox caused CK scenario? the thought of the foundation making Santa's sleigh is hilarious, and the time travel is the icing on the cake, although the bootstrap paradox is quite a big hole in the article, but other than that I love this.

also I know series 1 cross testing is taboo but if I remember correctly SCP-239 created Santa Claus when she was told it was just a story, so maybe Santa Claus was always just a story until 239 made it retrocausalicly real, making a solution to the bootstrap paradox by sort of 'forcing' the universe to be on a set track, also just now I remember one of the researchers saying that 239 made Santa Claus is 'uncontainable' which makes sense with 4255 and all the timey wimey stuff. so that's my headcanon.
(sorry for the wall of text, oof)

woo

Thanks everyone! I'm planning on doing a small rewrite to this, make it flow a bit better, clean up some dialogue, add some CSS stuffs, so stay tuned!

EDIT: I did some of the updates. I still need to make an image and touch up some other stuff, but this looks good so far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Efforts to partake in a secondary interview with SCP-4255 are to be attempted, as SCP-4255 seems to possess valuable information on controllable temporal anomalies and on the historical future of the human race.

this should've been updated after Addendum 4255-2: right? since they figured out the anomoaly was "foundation-made".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ho ho holy shit, this was well written. Take my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My favorite Christmas SCP.*



*That I've read so far…

item.title: Killed the anomalous apostrophe in the interview log for ya
item.content:encoded:
Changed

Get's boring after a while you know?

in

SCP-4255: Oh, um, they're a bunch of people who regulate time stuff, and they kinda made me do this, you see I made myself a time machine, and started going back in time to steal cool stuff, I had a block from those pyramids that were in Africa, I got a painting that Hitler made, things like that. But one day they just came in and arrested me, I don't know how they knew but they did. So now they are making me do this for my punishment. I've been doing it for about 2 years now, one or two Christmases each day. Get's boring after a while you know?

to

Gets boring after a while you know?

Every day, poor apostrophes are ruthlessly -Neutralized by unknowing Foundation researchers trying to pluralize acronyms and write funny little articles. Please stop the apostrophe abuse. Apostrophe's are important!

In all seriousness though if I broke some rule or you want me to change it back, I will. I just figure a typo wouldn't be part of something the guy actually said.

Edit: It probably isn't clear, but by having an apostrophe you're saying that there's a never before mentioned character with the name of "Get", and that SCP-4255 is asking the researcher about the "boring after a while you know" owned by Get, aka "Get's boring after a while you know".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
thanks for the meme idea
i mean SCP-4666 is better hands down so

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember seeing this in the crit forums a while back and giving a far less polished version a quick peek. I must say, you've improved this significantly since I last saw it. I enjoyed it a lot, and since it's almost that time of year again, this was great timing as well!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The real question is who would win in a fight between him and SCP-4666. There can be only one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty decent article, and a major improvement from when I first saw it in the sandbox. However, the only thing requiring criticism are some grammatical errors here and there in the article - should be easy to fix - and personally I don’t like the final addendum.

The concept of the Foundation causing Santa Clause is interesting, however I feel like it’s a cliche move in time travel stories and, of course, leads to a mind-numbing temporal paradox.

Otherwise it’s a damn good article, and I will still upvote it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was wonderful. Absolutely wonderful. I was upvoting when I knew it was just Santa Claus, and then after the interview log I was cracking up. Well done. Upvoted with gusto.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Solid SCP. Makes a modicum of sense, which is as much as you can hope for when trying to explain the physics of Santa Claus. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Possibly one of my favourite SCP's. The only complaint I have is the small number of instances. Otherwise, it was a great read
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really fun read, just one nitpicky thing. Thanksgiving doesn't always fall on November 22nd but rather the fourth Thursday of November, so if that 's what you were going for with the time period of containment it may warrant an edit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't believe that the future Foundation would create Santa Clause, making so much work for their past's elves

Fixed that typo for ya :p

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My guess is that they are forced to keep the Santa thing going because without it the Foundation possibly would not have figured out time-traveling Stan was a thing, and considering he pretty much is a history raider, they fear that having him uncontained for any longer could have caused another much dangerous paradox

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed this, it was incredibly wholesome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hah, you kids and all your newfangled time travel paradoxes *grumble* *grumble* ngl tho my head kinda hurts thinking about it.

The paradox approach is interesting, but outside of Stan Clane the Santa Claus he acted as doesn't really differ much from pop culture depiction—dare I say, the Santa Claus depicted is kinda boring. So no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I understand. The reason that they did it is because they did it, from a weird time travel standpoint. It's a bootstrap paradox yes, and I'd like to think that the SCP Foundation does a lot of weird stuff in order to stop time paradoxes like that. Time travel stuff can get complicated however, and I understand if I didn't write it well enough to get away with it.

I might try to fix up some of the numbers that make more sense for the, maybe calculate some stuff to see some more exact numbers. also, I like to think that they use time travel to cut down on the time it takes to do it. I may add more versions present, because now that I think about it, it does seem like a pretty small number. Anyway, thanks for the read!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry to downvote, I really am. I actually like the concept of making Santa an actual SCP, but I can't believe that the future Foundation would create Santa Clause, making so much work for their past selves. I know it's to prevent a paradox and potential reality shift, but this is already sort of a bootstrap paradox. They only do it because they already did it, but where did this start? Why would they do this in the first place (if a time loop can be said to have such a thing)?

You also state that he travels at a hundred kph and has been known split into up to 47 simultaneous instances, but 47 Santa's moving at 100 kph is still not enough to visit every Christmas Celebrating household in one night.

But this was an overall decent enough SCP, so please don't be discouraged by my downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well written and a lot of fun to read. +1

item.title: Santa Claus, The Time Traveller
item.content:encoded:
First SCP posted yay

Thanks to not_a_seagull and GhastKarp for the critique on this, I couldn't have done this without them.

Now let's just hope this doesn't get downvoted into oblivion.
